description:
Add the `workerExecution` configuration block that controls which execution substrate is used for new workers.

## What to implement

In `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/config.ts`:

```ts
workerExecution: {
  mode: "current-branch" | "worktree";  // default: "worktree" initially (flipped in Phase 5)
  maxLifetime: number | null;           // ms, default: null (no limit) — crash detection timeout
  allowDetachedHead: boolean;           // default: false — current-branch launch safety valve
  review: {
    enabled: boolean;                   // default: true — per-worker review for current-branch
  };
}
```

Environment overrides:
- `PI_BEADWORK_WORKER_EXECUTION_MODE=current-branch|worktree`
- `PI_BEADWORK_WORKER_MAX_LIFETIME=300000` (ms, optional; empty/unset means `null`)
- `PI_BEADWORK_WORKER_ALLOW_DETACHED_HEAD=1|0|true|false`
- `PI_BEADWORK_WORKER_REVIEW_ENABLED=1|0|true|false`

## Why `allowDetachedHead` exists

Current-branch worker identity normally depends on recording a branch name at launch and rejecting detached HEAD. Some advanced or CI-style workflows may intentionally run in detached HEAD. Support that only through an explicit opt-in config/env override so the default remains safe and unsurprising.

When `allowDetachedHead = false`:
- `prepareCurrentBranchCheckout` rejects `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returning `HEAD`.

When `allowDetachedHead = true`:
- launch may proceed;
- `branchName` should record a clear sentinel such as `HEAD`;
- `launchHead` is still required;
- verification evidence must include that the worker launched detached, because branch-drift checks cannot be interpreted the same way.

## Design Rules

- Default to `"worktree"` during development (Phase 5 flips to `"current-branch"`).
- Env override takes precedence over config file.
- `maxLifetime` is for crash detection timeout; `null` means rely only on supervisor polling and explicit process/tmux exit checks.
- `allowDetachedHead` defaults to `false`; current-branch detached HEAD support is opt-in only.
- `review.enabled` controls per-worker review for current-branch mode ONLY.
- Existing `worktrees` config remains but applies ONLY when mode is `worktree`.
- Do NOT let worktree-specific config (`copyFiles`, `setupCommands`, `cleanup`, `rerunSetupOnReuse`) leak into current-branch behavior.
- `landing.review.enabled` is a SEPARATE setting for worktree landing review — it must NOT control current-branch review.

## Isolation Semantics

When `mode = "current-branch"`:
- `worktrees.copyFiles` → ignored
- `worktrees.setupCommands` → ignored
- `worktrees.cleanup` → ignored
- `worktrees.rerunSetupOnReuse` → ignored
- `landing.review.enabled` → does NOT control current-branch review (separate concern)
- `workerExecution.review.enabled` → DOES control current-branch review
- `allowDetachedHead` → only affects whether launch can proceed when git reports detached HEAD

When `mode = "worktree"`:
- `workerExecution.review.enabled` → ignored (worktree uses `landing.review.enabled`)
- `workerExecution.allowDetachedHead` → ignored (worktree branch handling stays existing behavior)
- All existing worktree config applies as before

## Tests

- Config loads defaults correctly (`mode: "worktree"`, `maxLifetime: null`, `allowDetachedHead: false`, `review.enabled: true`).
- Env override works for mode.
- `maxLifetime` parses null/empty/unset and numeric values; invalid values produce clear config errors.
- `allowDetachedHead` parses file/env values and defaults to false.
- `workerExecution.review.enabled` parses file/env values and remains independent from landing review.
- Worktree config is not consulted when mode is `current-branch`.
- `landing.review.enabled = false` does NOT affect `workerExecution.review.enabled`.
- Both review settings are independent.

## Definition of Done

- Config block loads from file and env.
- Type-safe access to `config.workerExecution.mode`, `.maxLifetime`, `.allowDetachedHead`, `.review.enabled`.
- Isolation rules enforced at config resolution time and launch callsites.
- Tests cover all cases.
- lint/test/typecheck pass clean.

comments.text: Implemented workerExecution config block and committed e8cc029. Targeted config tests pass: npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension -- src/__tests__/unit/config.test.ts. Did not close because package lint/typecheck are currently blocked by unrelated in-progress changes outside .1.2 scope: src/orchestrator.ts has formatting/unused import lint failures and typecheck fails in current-branch/worktree runtime callsites (actions/orchestrator/registry/tui), plus protected dirty orchestrator.test.ts remains untouched.

title: Add workerExecution config block with mode and maxLifetime
